---
title: "5 plots"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}

rm(list = ls())

library(ggplot2)
library(reshape2)

# Number of size classes
m <- 57
hours <- 24
# min cell vol
v_min <- 2^(-5)
#spacing between size classes on log2 scale 
delta_v <- 0.125
size_index <- 1:m

#Actual cell vol
v_i <- v_min * 2^((size_index - 1) * delta_v)

#true parameters from sosik
gamma_max_true <- 0.20
delta_max_true <- 0.03
E_star_true <- 500
a_true <- 2
b_true <- 2

#storing true paramters in a vector form
true_base <- c(
  gamma_max = gamma_max_true,
  delta_max = delta_max_true,
  E_star    = E_star_true,
  a         = a_true,
  b         = b_true
)

#Light function 
E_t <- function(t) {
  1000 * max(0, sin(2 * pi * t / 24))
}

#Growth fucntion 
gamma_t <- function(t, gamma_max, E_star) {
  gamma_max * (1 - exp(-E_t(t) / E_star))
}

#Diision fucntion 
delta_i_t <- function(t, delta_max, a, b) {
  if (t < 6) return(rep(0, m))
  
  delta_i <- delta_max * ((a * v_i)^b / (1 + (a * v_i)^b))
  return(delta_i)
}

#one small time step transition matrix
matrix_A <- function(t, gamma_max, delta_max, E_star, a, b) {
  #empty column filled with 0
  A <- matrix(0, nrow = m, ncol = m)

#Growth rate at t
  gamma <- gamma_t(t, gamma_max, E_star)
#division rate for all size classes at time t 
  delta_i <- delta_i_t(t, delta_max, a, b)
  
  j <- 1 + (1 / delta_v)

#Growth term for the matrix A, from i to i+1
  for (i in 1:(m - 1)) {
    A[i + 1, i] <- gamma * (1 - delta_i[i])
  }

#Largest size class cannnot move after m 
#Growing cells in class m stay in m 
  A[m, m] <- A[m, m] + gamma * (1 - delta_i[m])

#Division term for small size classes 
#Some dividing cells produce daughters that go into size class 1
  for (i in 2:(j - 1)) {
    A[1, i] <- A[1, i] + 2 * delta_i[i]
  }
  #Diviison for larger size classes 
  for (i in j:m) {
    smaller_class <- i + 1 - j
    A[smaller_class, i] <- A[smaller_class, i] + 2 * delta_i[i]
  }
  #Stasis - ceels that don't grow or divide 
  A[1, 1] <- A[1, 1] + ((1 - gamma) * (1 - delta_i[1])) + 2 * delta_i[1]

#stasis 
  for (i in 2:(m - 1)) {
    A[i, i] <- A[i, i] + (1 - gamma) * (1 - delta_i[i])
  }
  #Stasis for largest size clases
  A[m, m] <- A[m, m] + (1 - delta_i[m])
  
  return(A)
}

#One hour transition matrix 
#multiply Matrix A 6 times 
matrix_B <- function(t, gamma_max, delta_max, E_star, a, b, dt = 1/6) {

#start with indentity matrix 
  B <- diag(m)
#number of small time step inside one hour 
  n_steps <- 1 / dt

  #loop through the smaller time steps 
  for (k in 0:(n_steps - 1)) {
    #current time 
    time_now <- t + k * dt

# The transitin matrix at current time 
    A_k <- matrix_A(
      t = time_now,
      gamma_max = gamma_max,
      delta_max = delta_max,
      E_star = E_star,
      a = a,
      b = b
    )
    
    B <- A_k %*% B
  }
  
  return(B)
}

#Initial size distribution 
#creates bell shaped distribution centered around size 20
w0 <- dnorm(size_index, mean = 20, sd = 6)
#normalization so the proportion sums to 1 
w0 <- w0 / sum(w0)

#It takes the parameter vector and then generates full 24 hour true distribution 
simulate_model <- function(params) {
  #extracting all the parameters 
  gamma_max <- params[1]
  delta_max <- params[2]
  E_star <- params[3]
  a <- params[4]
  b <- params[5]

#create an empty matrix to store the distribution for every hour 
  W <- matrix(0, nrow = hours, ncol = m)
#put the initial distribution in the first row 
  W[1, ] <- w0
  # Move the distribution forward from hour 1 to hour 24
  for (t in 1:(hours - 1)) {
    # one hour transition matrix for hour t 
    B_t <- matrix_B(
      t = t,
      gamma_max = gamma_max,
      delta_max = delta_max,
      E_star = E_star,
      a = a,
      b = b
    )
    # Predicting the next hour distribution 
    W[t + 1, ] <- B_t %*% W[t, ]
    #normalisation 
    W[t + 1, ] <- W[t + 1, ] / sum(W[t + 1, ])
  }
  
  return(W)
}

#simulatign the true model using the true values of params 
W_true <- simulate_model(true_base)

#random sampling 
set.seed(123)

#total number of sampled cell at each hour 
N <- 1000000
#empty matrix to store fake cell counts 
Counts <- matrix(0, nrow = hours, ncol = m)

#fake observed counts at each hour 
for (t in 1:hours) {
  Counts[t, ] <- rmultinom(
    n = 1,
    size = N,
    prob = W_true[t, ]
  )
}

#counts into obs proportion 
W_obs <- Counts / rowSums(Counts)

# Guess model 
# Move the distribution forward from hour 1 to hour 24
# starts from first obs row 
guess_model <- function(params, W) {
  
  gamma_max <- params[1]
  delta_max <- params[2]
  E_star <- params[3]
  a <- params[4]
  b <- params[5]

#empty matrix for predicted distribution 
  W_pred <- matrix(0, nrow = hours, ncol = m)
#start prediction from the first obs distribution 
  W_pred[1, ] <- W[1, ]

#predict forward through tieme 
  for (t in 1:(hours - 1)) {
    
    B_t <- matrix_B(
      t = t,
      gamma_max = gamma_max,
      delta_max = delta_max,
      E_star = E_star,
      a = a,
      b = b
    )
    #predict next hour using the obs current row 
    W_pred[t + 1, ] <- B_t %*% W[t, ]
    W_pred[t + 1, ] <- W_pred[t + 1, ] / sum(W_pred[t + 1, ])
  }
  
  return(W_pred)
}
#objective fucntion 
#This is what optim tries to minimize 
#It measures the total sqrd differences between obs and predicted distribution 
objective_function <- function(params, W) {
  
  gamma_max <- params[1]
  delta_max <- params[2]
  E_star <- params[3]
  a <- params[4]
  b <- params[5]
  # If parametrs are outside allowed range, return huge error 
  if (gamma_max < 0 || gamma_max > 0.5 ||
      delta_max < 0 || delta_max > 0.15 ||
      E_star <= 0 ||
      a <= 0 ||
      b <= 0) {
    return(1e10)
  }
  #predict distribution using guess param 
  W_pred <- guess_model(params, W)
# errors 
  error <- sum((W - W_pred)^2)
  
  return(error)
}

#Use the true params as initial guess for opti
initial_guess <- true_base

fit <- optim(
  par = initial_guess,
  fn = objective_function,
  W = W_obs,
  method = "L-BFGS-B",
  lower = c(0, 0, 100, 0.1, 0.1),
  upper = c(0.5, 0.15, 5000, 30, 10)
)
#Extract the estimated params from the optimizer output 
estimated_params <- fit$par
#Giving the esti params the same names as true params 
names(estimated_params) <- names(true_base)
#Result table comparing true and estimated param values 
results <- data.frame(
  Parameter = names(true_base),
  True = true_base,
  Estimated = estimated_params,
  PercentError = 100 * (estimated_params - true_base) / true_base
)

print(results)

#Heatmaps 
#turns distributin matrix to a heatmap 
plot_heatmap <- function(W, title_name) {
  #convert matrix W into long format data frame 
  df <- melt(W)
  # rename column for clarity
  colnames(df) <- c("Hour", "SizeClass", "Proportion")

  #hour index changing so that first row is hour 0
  df$Hour <- df$Hour - 1

  #add actual cell volume corresponding to each size class 
  df$CellVolume <- v_i[df$SizeClass]

#create the heatmap
  ggplot(df, aes(x = CellVolume, y = Hour, fill = Proportion)) +
    geom_tile() +
    scale_x_log10() +
    scale_fill_gradientn(colors = c("blue", "cyan", "yellow", "red")) +
    labs(
      title = title_name,
      x = expression("Cell volume ("*mu*m^3*")"),
      y = "Hour after dawn",
      fill = "Proportion"
    ) +
    theme_minimal()
}
#Fitted distribution using estimated params
W_fit <- guess_model(estimated_params, W_obs)

print(plot_heatmap(W_true, "True Model Distribution"))
print(plot_heatmap(W_obs, "Fake Observed Distribution"))
print(plot_heatmap(W_fit, "Fitted Model Distribution"))


# PARAMETER RECOVERY: one parameter changes, four fixed
param_ranges <- list(
  gamma_max = c(0.10, 0.15, 0.20, 0.25, 0.30),
  delta_max = c(0.01, 0.02, 0.03, 0.05, 0.08),
  E_star    = c(100, 300, 500, 700, 1000),
  a         = c(0.5, 1, 2, 3, 4),
  b         = c(0.5, 1, 2, 3, 4)
)


# percent error 
percent_error <- function(estimated, true) {
  (100 * (estimated - true) / true)
}
#empty data frame to store all the sensitivity results 
all_results <- data.frame()

#Loop over each params that will be varied 
for (param_name in names(param_ranges)) {
  #loop over true value for that param
  for (param_value in param_ranges[[param_name]]) {

    #start from true param vector 
    true_params <- true_base
    #replace the paramter that is only currently being varied 
    true_params[param_name] <- param_value

    #simulate the true distribution for this parameter set 
    W_true_sens <- simulate_model(true_params)

 #each sensitivity run uses reproducible fake data
    set.seed(123)
    #empty matrix 
    Counts <- matrix(0, nrow = hours, ncol = m)
    #fake obs counts for sensitivity run
    for (t in 1:hours) {
      Counts[t, ] <- rmultinom(
        n = 1,
        size = N,
        prob = W_true_sens[t, ]
      )
    }
    
    W_obs_sens <- Counts / rowSums(Counts)
    #fit the model to this fake obs sensitivity dataset 
    fit_sens <- optim(
      par = initial_guess,
      fn = objective_function,
      W = W_obs_sens,
      method = "L-BFGS-B",
      lower = c(0, 0, 100, 0.1, 0.1),
      upper = c(0.5, 0.15, 5000, 30, 10)
    )
    
    estimated_sens <- fit_sens$par
    names(estimated_sens) <- names(true_base)
    
    errors <- percent_error(estimated_sens, true_params)
    #store result 
    temp <- data.frame(
      VariedParameter = param_name,
      VariedValue = param_value,
      EstimatedParameter = names(errors),
      TrueValue = as.numeric(true_params),
      EstimatedValue = as.numeric(estimated_sens),
      PercentError = as.numeric(errors),
      #for convergence 
      ObjectiveValue = fit_sens$value,
      Convergence = fit_sens$convergence
    )
    
    all_results <- rbind(all_results, temp)
    
    print(paste("Finished:", param_name, "=", param_value))
  }
}

print(all_results)
#Modification one Convergence test
# Check how many optimizer runs converged
table(all_results$Convergence)

# Print cases where optim did not converge
bad_convergence <- subset(all_results, Convergence != 0)
print(bad_convergence)

write.csv(
  all_results,
  "parameter_recovery_sensitivity_results.csv",
  row.names = FALSE
)


# 5 sensitivity plots


make_sensitivity_plot <- function(parameter_name) {
  
  plot_data <- subset(all_results,
                      VariedParameter == parameter_name)
  #addition for convergence 
   plot_data$ConvergenceStatus <- ifelse (
    plot_data$Convergence == 0,
    "Converged",
    "Did not converge"
  )
  ggplot(
    plot_data,
    aes(
      x = VariedValue,
      y = PercentError,
      color = EstimatedParameter,
      group = EstimatedParameter,
      shape = ConvergenceStatus
    )
  ) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
    geom_point(size = 2) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0,
               linetype = "dashed") +
    scale_shape_manual(
      values = c(
        "Converged" = 16,
        "Did not converge" = 1
      )
    
    labs(
      title = paste("Parameter recovery for",
                    parameter_name),
      x = paste("True value of",
                parameter_name),
      y = "Percent error (%)",
      color = "Estimated parameter",
      shape = "Optimizer status"
    ) +
    theme_minimal()
}
p_gamma <- make_sensitivity_plot("gamma_max")
p_delta <- make_sensitivity_plot("delta_max")
p_E     <- make_sensitivity_plot("E_star")
p_a     <- make_sensitivity_plot("a")
p_b     <- make_sensitivity_plot("b")

print(p_gamma)
print(p_delta)
print(p_E)
print(p_a)
print(p_b)

ggsave("sensitivity_gamma_max.png", p_gamma, width = 8, height = 5)
ggsave("sensitivity_delta_max.png", p_delta, width = 8, height = 5)
ggsave("sensitivity_E_star.png", p_E, width = 8, height = 5)
ggsave("sensitivity_a.png", p_a, width = 8, height = 5)
ggsave("sensitivity_b.png", p_b, width = 8, height = 5)
```

You can add options to executable code like this

```{r}
#| echo: false
2 * 2
```

The `echo: false` option disables the printing of code (only output is displayed).
